mnemonics: STRUCTure Blueprint: Just like an architect needs a blueprint (struct declaration) before building (creating variables), you must declare a struct before creating variables of that type.
keyPoints: A struct declaration defines a new data type by bundling together variables (members) of different types under one name.
Declaring a struct (e.g., `struct Person { ... };`) does not allocate any memory; it only creates a template or blueprint.
The struct definition must end with a semicolon after the closing brace.
commonMistakes: Forgetting the semicolon at the end of the struct declaration, which causes a compiler error.
Trying to use the struct members directly without first creating a variable of that struct type.
